triageFlags.title: Excessive Debt-to-Income Ratio
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to unsustainable affordability.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum DTI of 40% for Tier B applicants, but the calculated DTI is 11000%, indicating a severe affordability issue.

triageFlags.title: Income Discrepancy Detected
triageFlags.description: The reported net income is implausibly low and inconsistent with the applicant's employment status.
triageFlags.recommendation: Request verified income documentation to resolve the discrepancy.
triageFlags.reasoning: Policy mandates income verification for affordability assessments. The reported income is not credible, necessitating further investigation.

content.reasoning: The analysis identified a DTI ratio far exceeding the policy threshold for the applicant's credit tier, alongside an income discrepancy that undermines the reliability of affordability calculations. These factors collectively render the proposal unviable.
